comment: Quality Gates catalog -- docs-build dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/docs-build/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/docs-build/.

asserts: mkdocs build --strict exits 0 (catches broken cross-refs, missing nav entries, broken admonition syntax)
mkdocs build log does not contain 'Syntax error in text' (mermaid HTML-entity bug guard per POLISH-03)
rendered HTML site does not contain 'Syntax error in text' (mermaid leaked-error guard per POLISH-03 regression mode)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: mkdocs build failed --strict OR mermaid HTML-entity regression -- inspect mkdocs.yml + recent docs/ edits + the build log path under /tmp/check-docs-site*/build.log

asserts: every docs/**/*.md page that source-references a triple-backtick mermaid fence has a corresponding playwright artifact at .planning/verifications/playwright/<section>/<slug>.json
each artifact's svg_counts length equals mermaid_count (no missing diagrams)
each artifact's svg_counts contains no zeros (no empty-render diagrams)
each artifact's console_errors is empty (no browser-side rendering failures)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a docs page added a mermaid fence without a matching playwright artifact OR an artifact got stale -- refresh via the playwright MCP walk per CLAUDE.md 'Whole-nav-section playwright walks' section

asserts: every relative Markdown link in docs/*.md (top-level: index.md, roadmap.md, security.md, why.md) + docs/{concepts/*.md, tutorials/*.md, how-it-works/*.md, guides/*.md, reference/*.md, benchmarks/*.md} + .planning/PROJECT.md resolves to an existing file path
http(s):// and mailto: and pure-anchor links are skipped (anchor validation is mkdocs --strict's job per the existing script docstring)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a docs page references a deleted file OR a moved-without-redirect file -- run python3 quality/gates/docs-build/link-resolution.py locally for the BROKEN-list

asserts: every badge URL extracted from README.md badge block (CI, Quality (weekly), Docs, codecov, License MIT, Rust stable, reposix-cli on crates.io) HEADs to HTTP 200
every badge URL extracted from docs/index.md badge block (CI, Quality (weekly)) HEADs to HTTP 200
every badge URL's response Content-Type contains 'image' (svg, png, etc.)

the new QG-09 endpoint badge URL https://reubenjohn.github.io/reposix/badge.json HEADs to HTTP 200 with Content-Type containing 'json' (added in P60 Wave F; pre-Wave-F this assert is documented and the URL is in WAVE_F_PENDING_URLS until Wave F lands the publish)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a badge URL is 404 or returns non-image content-type -- fix the badge URL in README.md or docs/index.md OR substitute a current shields.io equivalent. Common: shields.io drift, codecov project rename, badge-URL typos.

sources: .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md:487-508 (badges-resolve FAILs on pre-push — real-vs-transient determination, MEDIUM/P2)

asserts: badges-resolve.py has been re-run in isolation on >=2 spaced occasions and the pass/fail pattern recorded (the determination artifact distinguishes a transient shields.io/Codecov flake from a genuinely broken badge URL)
the real-vs-transient verdict is recorded and the GOOD-TO-HAVES.md badges-resolve entry (L487-508) is RESOLVED with that finding (status flipped from OPEN)
if GENUINELY BROKEN: the offending badge URL in README.md/docs/index.md is fixed (or substituted with a current shields.io equivalent)
if TRANSIENT: badges-resolve.py gains a retry/backoff before failing OR a documented waiver note is added, so the check reaches brightgreen reliably
net: python3 quality/gates/docs-build/badges-resolve.py exits 0 (docs-build/badges-resolve is GREEN, no longer FAILing pre-push)

rows.owner_hint: NOT DONE this session (catalog-first SPEC mint). During the P94-P97 debt-drain window: re-run `python3 quality/gates/docs-build/badges-resolve.py` >=2x spaced apart, record which badge (if any) flaps. If a specific URL is consistently 404/non-image -> fix it in README.md/docs/index.md. If it flaps -> add retry/backoff to badges-resolve.py (or a documented waiver note). Then close the GOOD-TO-HAVES badges-resolve entry with the verdict.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier confirms >=2 spaced isolated re-runs happened, that a real-vs-transient verdict was recorded and the GOOD-TO-HAVES entry resolved, and that badges-resolve.py now exits 0. If the description's claim ('the badge failure was diagnosed and fixed-or-waived, not left as an unexplained recurring red') were false — e.g. the entry was closed WITHOUT a determination, or badges-resolve.py still exits non-zero, or a genuinely-broken URL was papered over with a waiver instead of fixed — the exit-0 assert and the entry-resolved assert would fail concretely. Doc-honesty/build gate (docs-build dimension).
